## CISD Naloxone (Narcan) Program & Senate Bill 629

### Background
Chris McCord provided an overview of naloxone/Narcan:
- **Naloxone** is an opioid antagonist that binds to opioid receptors in the brain with greater affinity than opioids, effectively reversing opioid overdose effects (particularly severe respiratory distress)
- **Narcan** is the brand name of naloxone used by the district
- Effects are short-lived (a few minutes), serving as a bridge until EMS arrives
- Administered nasally; works in approximately 15 seconds
- Very minimal to no side effects if administered incorrectly (i.e., person is not actually experiencing an opioid overdose)

### Current Status (2022–2023 School Year)
- Narcan has been deployed at **all secondary campuses** (grades 7–12) throughout the 2022–2023 school year
- Available in school clinics, athletic training rooms (where applicable), and carried by each CISD police officer (two-dose packs)
- **Narcan has already been used once** on a CISD campus — McCord stated he could not definitively prove it saved the student's life but that "it made a huge difference" while waiting for 911 response
- Credit given to Barbara Robertson (Director of Nursing) and Chief Blakelock for acquisition and deployment
- Montgomery County has been a significant partner, providing doses at no cost; recently provided 72 additional doses through Homeland Security

### Senate Bill 629
- **Signed by Governor Abbott on June 18, 2023**
- Effective September 2023
- **Requires** school districts to maintain and be able to administer an opioid antagonist for **grades 6 and below**
- Requires at least one authorized and trained individual per campus
- Provides for possible funding

### CISD's Expansion Plan
- CISD was **already ahead of SB 629** requirements, having had Narcan at secondary campuses for a full year before the bill
- Intermediate campuses were already being supplied before the bill was ratified
- **The district plans to expand Narcan to ALL campuses, including elementary schools**, going beyond the SB 629 requirement of 6th grade and below
- **Rationale:** People of all ages are on elementary campuses; children may have access to pills that appear to be ordinary medication but contain fentanyl
- Chief Blakelock noted that prescription pills confiscated from students this year tested positive for fentanyl at the state lab — incidents were limited but confirmed
- Full deployment anticipated by **August 2023** for the start of the school year
- Nurses and athletic trainers have had a positive attitude toward the program

### Key Safety Point
- Under the law, a nurse or trained individual who **reasonably suspects** an opioid overdose may administer Narcan
- Each officer carries a two-dose pack; it is possible both doses may need to be administered before EMS arrives

## Required Psychological First Aid Training

**Presented by Ethan Barton**

- Texas Education Code §37.108(f)(6)(C)(i) requires members of the District Safety and Security Committee to complete **psychological first aid training**
- Barton researched available courses and identified a manageable option approved by the Safety Center
- Barton has already completed the training himself
- **Training details will be emailed** to all committee members (via Linda Godfrey) with instructions
- Training is self-paced/on your own time; includes a test and certificate upon completion
- Completion is on the **honor system**, but members should retain certificates in case the state requests them
- No firm deadline, but members are asked to complete it **as soon as possible**
- Barton offered to assist any committee member who has questions

- The committee is required to meet at least once per semester (fall, spring) and once in summer

- Timing depends on results of ongoing **intruder detection audits** — if a finding occurs, the committee has **45 working days** to convene
- At least **one meeting will occur during the Fall 2023 semester**
- McCord noted the committee will continue to bring new topics and revisit/expand on current initiatives (e.g., Narcan program)
